xposts.title: Self model registers reactions to qualia in protocol
xposts.excerpt: Qualia are feature vectors of a present world model, presented to the simulation of a unified and motivated real-time observer. They change the dynamics of the self model. The self model registers its reaction to qualia, and maintains a protocol that it can access.

xposts.title: Consciousness organizes scene via observing self model
xposts.excerpt: Consciousness (in some sense of the word) is organizing the current scene representation, and recording and selectively reactivating the reactions of an observing self model, in a protocol that allows the recreation of partial scenes and reactions.

xposts.title: Human self model as anthropomorphic simulacrum
xposts.excerpt: Our human self model is an anthropomorphic simulacrum, into which perception and motivation are being projected.

xposts.title: Agency and consciousness may be independent
xposts.excerpt: I am not sure what you mean by "unification" and what your referents of body and mind are. I sometimes experience conscious states that do not involve my body or models thereof. Consciousness is also not necessarily agentic. Agency and consciousness may be independent.

xposts.title: Few people have agency; possessed by ideologies
xposts.excerpt: Few people in this game have agency. Instead, they are possessed by ideologies that constantly evolve and pull their energy from the opposition they create.

xposts.title: Agency varies in degrees with awareness and lucidity
xposts.excerpt: Consciousness does not seem to come in degrees (but awareness, integration, lucidity and agency do). Either “the lights are on” or off.

terms.title: self model
terms.definition: An anthropomorphic simulacrum into which perception and motivation are projected; it registers reactions to qualia as feature vectors from a world model and maintains a protocol for accessing partial scene recreations. The self model is simulated as a unified, motivated real-time observer whose dynamics are altered by qualia.

terms.title: agency
terms.definition: A variable degree of control and autonomy that can be independent from consciousness; often lacking when individuals are possessed by evolving ideologies, but can form self-reinforcing first-person nexuses detached from particular substrates.
